englishDefinition: Feeling very happy and full of strong feelings about something.
Making people feel happy and full of strong feelings.
A reason given to explain why something was done or not done.
To forgive someone for a small mistake.
To do physical activity to keep the body fit and strong.
A door or way to leave a place.
To think that something will happen.
A belief that something will happen or be true.
Costing a lot of money.
Knowledge or skill that comes from doing something many times.

A person with a lot of knowledge or skill in a special area.
To tell someone about something so that they can understand.
A statement that helps someone understand something.
To send goods to another country to be sold.
To show what you think or feel using words or actions.
The look on someone's face that shows their feelings.
More than is usual or necessary.
One of two body parts in the head used to see.
To turn toward something, or to deal with a problem.
A building or equipment that helps people do something.
Something that is known to be true.
A large building where workers and machines make things.

The state of not succeeding, or a person or thing that does not succeed.
Treating everyone the same way, without giving any special favors.
To move quickly down toward the ground.
To begin sleeping.
To begin to feel romantic love for someone.
Not correct; not true.
A group of people who are related, like parents and their children.
Known by very many people.
A person who likes someone or something very much.
Extremely good; wonderful.
At a long distance away.
The money you pay to travel by bus, train, or taxi.
An area of land used for growing crops or raising animals.
A person who grows crops or raises animals on land.
A popular style of clothes, hair, or behavior at a certain time.
Following a style that is popular at the moment.
A male parent.
A device that controls the flow of water from a pipe.
Responsibility for a mistake or wrong action.

Liked the most among similar things.

Money paid for a service or for entering a place.
To give food to a person or animal.
To have a particular emotion or sense in the body.
An emotion such as happiness, anger, or fear.
Belonging to the sex that can have babies.
A special day or time when people celebrate something.
A body temperature higher than normal, often caused by sickness.
A small number; not many.
A piece of open land for crops or sports.
To use the body or weapons to hurt someone in a quarrel.
To think about a problem until you understand or solve it.
To understand or solve a problem after thinking about it.
To put things into a container until there is no more space.
To do another person's job for a short time when they are away.
To write information in the empty spaces on a form.
A series of moving pictures that tell a story; a movie.
Last; coming at the end.
After a long time, especially a longer time than expected.

Good; pleasant; healthy.
One of the five long parts at the end of the hand.

Small paper tubes that explode loudly when burned, used in celebrations.
Solid and not easily moved or changed.
Coming before all others in time, order, or importance.

Smooth and level, without bumps or holes.

The flat surface inside a building that you walk on.

An illness with fever, sore throat, and tired body, like a bad cold.

The center of attention or interest.

Full of thick mist, making it hard to see far.

Coming next or after.
Things that people and animals eat.
